scope: paraphrase_human alias-leak removal and forbidden-neighbor tightening for fetch credentials vs cookie scope
learner_facing_quality_improvement: The fetch-vs-cookie-scope paraphrase now behaves like a real learner question instead of leaking the canonical fetch term, and eval will reject one-sided CORS or cookie-scope primers that do not explain the comparison the learner actually asked for.

verification.alias_leak_audit: prompt no longer contains the canonical token `fetch` while reformulated_query keeps the corpus-facing vocabulary

notes: This wave keeps the learner prompt natural while preserving the canonical retrieval phrase only in reformulated_query.
The forbidden set is intentionally comparison-specific: single-axis cookie or CORS primers should not count as a win for this paraphrase.
Pilot-locked corpus docs were not modified.
